content.question: How much of the observed performance gap between gimnázium and vocational-track students at age 15 is attributable to the early (age 10-12) selection mechanism of 6/8-year programs versus the later (age 14) secondary tracking system or prior residential/peer segregation?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental or matched-cohort comparison of regions/cohorts with varying prevalence of 6/8-year programs while controlling for later track placement and baseline school segregation. Hungarian administrative data linking primary-school residence, 6/8-year entry, secondary track placement, and outcomes would enable this decomposition.
